pi_items.source: setup_task.sh line 14, export_result.sh line 14, ActivInspire format documentation
Combination of verified directory and filename from setup_task.sh and export_result.sh

Task description (task.json lines 5-6) states 'six types: Lever, Wheel and Axle, Pulley, Inclined Plane, Wedge, Screw'. Web search (Britannica/Wikipedia) confirms the six classical simple machines include 'wheel and axle' not just 'wheel'.

Task description (task.json line 6): definition text 'A rigid bar resting on a pivot' and text labels 'Fulcrum' and 'Load'. Web search on lever definition confirms 'rigid bar' and 'pivot' are standard terminology.
Task description (task.json line 6): definition text 'A flat supporting surface tilted at an angle' and text label 'Ramp'. Web search on inclined plane definition confirms these are standard terms.
Task description (task.json line 6): Page 2 requires 'a triangle for the fulcrum' (1 triangle); Page 3 requires 'a triangle for the ramp' (1 triangle). Total: 2 triangles minimum.
Task description (task.json line 6): Page 2 requires 'a rectangle/line for the beam'. Total: 1 rectangle/line minimum.

Task description (task.json lines 5-6): Page 2 diagram uses 'a triangle for the fulcrum, a rectangle/line for the beam, and a shape for the load' (3 shapes); Page 3 diagram uses 'a triangle for the ramp and a shape for the object' (2 shapes). Total: 5 shapes minimum.

privileged_info_summary: ActivInspire flipchart files use the .flipchart extension and are stored in /home/ga/Documents/Flipcharts. The target file is simple_machines_lesson.flipchart. A valid 3-page flipchart requires page 1 with title and six simple machine types, page 2 with a lever definition and diagram (minimum 1 triangle for fulcrum, 1 rectangle/line for beam, and 1 shape for load), and page 3 with an inclined plane definition and diagram (minimum 1 triangle for ramp and 1 shape for object). One metadata claim is factually incorrect: the list should include 'Wheel and Axle' (not just 'Wheel') as the official name of the second simple machine. All other metadata claims (file paths, required terms, shape minimums) are verified and accurate.
